content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as self-organizing curious ecosystems where each stored memory trace acts as an independent micro-agent that competes for retrieval based on environmental uncertainty, rather than being passive static entries in a centralized database. This turns the problem of optimizing memory retention for active learning into a curiosity-driven selection process that automatically prioritizes high-information, uncertainty-reducing memories.
